title: Drone video shows first snow of season at California's Big Bear Mountain Resort
creator: NBCLA
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- first exemplar added to the snow-capped mountain and winter alpine landscapes subgenre bucket, a subject not previously represented in this pack. A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/lbR7WSd_yI0); per this task's step 1 requirement it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-verified live in this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (curl 'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/lbR7WSd_yI0&format=json'), which returned title "Drone video shows first snow of season at California's Big Bear Mountain Resort", author_name 'NBCLA', author_url 'https://www.youtube.com/@NBCLA', and embed dimensions height=200/width=113 (portrait) -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's claimed title, creator, and vertical-Shorts framing, so this session's own primary-source check corroborates rather than merely repeats the prior agent's claim. This session additionally fetched the video's public thumbnail (https://i.ytimg.com/vi/lbR7WSd_yI0/hq2.jpg) to a session-scratchpad path outside this repo, visually inspected it once, and immediately deleted it -- confirming genuine drone footage of a snow-dusted alpine ski slope with evergreen trees, ski-lift towers in frame, and an on-screen creator-credit overlay reading "Lee Stockwell, Big Bear Mountain Resort" -- then discarded per the no-persist rule; no image or video file was retained anywhere in this repo or elsewhere on disk after this check. That credit overlay indicates the clip is resort/photographer-sourced footage licensed or repurposed by NBCLA for a local-news segment, not footage shot by a broadcaster's own aerial-news unit -- a distinction this record's score calibration accounts for (see below). No Creative Commons marking of any kind was present in the oEmbed response (no licensedForReuse/cc-by field), and a WebFetch attempt against the live Shorts page in this session returned no usable page text (JS-rendered content was truncated), so no CC marking could be positively confirmed there either. Per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), license_category is set to 'all-rights-reserved' -- consistent with NBCLA being a conventional broadcast-news channel with standard copyright. Consequently local_media_path stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. duration is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not carry a duration field, and no further verification of runtime (YouTube Data API call or manual playback) was attempted in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible Shorts-length number, this field stays unset. award_or_showcase_provenance is left null: this is a local-news weather/season segment repost of resort footage, not a formally juried contest or festival selection, and no award claim was found anywhere in the oEmbed metadata or the prior agent's research -- inventing one would violate the no-fabrication constraint. scores (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are scores_provenance: manually_estimated placeholder judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the spec's no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing ~0.70-0.85 range: sharpness=0.74 (the thumbnail shows crisp evergreen-tree and snow-texture detail consistent with a capable prosumer drone/camera, but the clip is a resort-sourced establishing shot re-hosted by a news outlet on YouTube Shorts, implying at least one extra re-compression pass versus a broadcaster's own in-house aerial unit -- scored a shade below abc-news-volcano-crater-lava-fpv.json's 0.77, which is genuinely broadcaster-shot); exposure=0.70 (the thumbnail shows a hazy, slightly overcast morning sky rendering close to blown-out white behind backlit tree silhouettes, against correctly-exposed snow and midground terrain -- a moderately high-dynamic-range scene typical of snow-and-sky compositions, with no HDR/log-profile claim found to offset it, scored near this pack's low-to-mid tier, comparable to lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json's 0.70 backlit case); motion_smoothness=0.78 (the framing and genre -- a steady establishing flyover along a ski slope for a seasonal b-roll segment -- is consistent with a smooth, deliberate push/pan rather than an erratic or handheld-feeling shot, scored at this pack's upper-middle tier similar to abc-news-volcano-crater-lava-fpv.json's 0.78, though no explicit gimbal/stabilization-software claim was verified for this specific clip). composite_score IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.74, exposure=0.70, motion_smoothness=0.78, composition=None) and drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights() via '.venv/bin/python3' in this session. default_weights() with no explicit version argument returned weights_version 'default-v2' (sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25 each); because composition's score is None (this pack's Milestone-1 Capability-1 convention), compute_composite_score's defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, so the result reduces to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.74+0.70+0.78)/3 = 0.7399999999999999 (rounded to 0.74 here), confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed. editorial_style: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in structural facts obtained via this session's own oEmbed fetch, not a guess from the title alone -- (1) the source URL itself is a /shorts/ path, YouTube's own platform-level designation for short-form vertical content; (2) the oEmbed response's embed dimensions (height=200, width=113) describe a portrait/vertical aspect ratio, inconsistent with the 16:9 landscape framing of this pack's long-form cinematic exemplars -- the same structural-evidence basis this pack already uses in abc-news-volcano-crater-lava-fpv.json and drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json. No live playback or scrubbing of the video itself was attempted in this session (only a single static thumbnail frame was fetched and immediately discarded, not the video stream), so review_method is set to 'text_provenance_only' rather than 'live_playback_review', and estimated_cut_count/avg_shot_length_seconds/transition_styles_observed stay null/empty rather than guessed, consistent with the prior agent's editorial-review pass finding no cut-count or shot-length detail in any source it consulted.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' inferred from two structural facts, not full playback: the /shorts/ URL path and the oEmbed embed's vertical 113x200 dimensions, both obtained via this session's own oEmbed fetch, plus a single discarded thumbnail frame confirming vertical framing and an on-screen creator-credit overlay. No browser-automation or playback tool was invoked to scrub or watch the video stream itself in this session, and the prior agent's editorial-review pass (which also did not perform live playback) found no cut-count, shot-length, or transition-style detail in the sources it consulted, so estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed stay null/empty rather than guessed. Given a punchy, single-location news b-roll segment is the described content, it plausibly has few internal cuts, but that is an inference from genre/format convention, not an observation, so it is not asserted as a specific cut count.
